Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. Mildly elevated ALT may indicate hepatic involvement. Further nephrological and diabetic evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
